title: Veg Hakka Noodles

subs.name: 200 g spaghetti boiled to al dente, or rice noodles for a gluten-free version
bean sprouts or thinly sliced mushrooms

ingredients.name: spring onion, whites and greens separated
sesame oil
visual_checks: for finishing; gingelly oil is the same as sesame oil
ingredients.notes: for finishing; gingelly oil is the same as sesame oil

subs.name: a few drops of any nut oil for the finishing aroma

steps.text: Boil a large pot of water with 1 tsp salt. Add the hakka noodles and cook according to pack directions — typically 4–5 minutes — until al dente; they should still have a faint bite. Drain immediately, run under cold water, and toss with 1 tsp oil to prevent sticking.

Prep all vegetables before heating the wok: shred cabbage finely, julienne the carrot, slice capsicum into thin strips, separate spring onion whites from greens. Everything goes in fast once the wok is hot, so nothing can wait.
Heat 3 tbsp oil in a wok or large kadai on the highest flame available until lightly smoking. Add garlic, ginger and green chilli and toss for 20 seconds until fragrant — do not let garlic colour.

Add sliced onion and spring onion whites and stir-fry for 1 minute. Add carrot strips next, stir for 30 seconds, then throw in cabbage and capsicum. Toss constantly on high heat for 2 minutes — the vegetables must stay slightly firm and bright.

Push the vegetables to one side. Add soy sauce, vinegar, chilli sauce and black pepper to the centre of the wok — let them sizzle for 10 seconds, then toss everything together to coat the vegetables evenly.

Add the drained noodles and toss vigorously using tongs or two ladles, lifting from the bottom so the noodles do not clump. Stir-fry for 2 minutes until the noodles are lightly toasted and fragrant. Adjust salt, drizzle sesame oil if using, and finish with spring onion greens.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: noodles → 200 g spaghetti boiled to al dente, or rice noodles for a gluten-free version; soy-sauce → coconut aminos; gingelly-oil → a few drops of any nut oil for the finishing aroma; cabbage → bean sprouts or thinly sliced mushrooms
indian_kitchen_adaptation: A large kadai on the highest gas burner works as well as a wok — the key is sufficient heat. Do not add extra water; let the high heat and soy sauce do the work.
